'''Matthew Ryan''' (born 13 June 1988) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[NRL]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Kingstone Press Championship]]. Ryan spent time on loan from the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].  Ryan currently plays for the [[Guildford Owls]] in the [[Ron Massey Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.theland.com.au/story/4905327/bulls-among-best/?cs=4963 |title=Bulls among best | the Land |access-date=3 October 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171004035853/http://www.theland.com.au/story/4905327/bulls-among-best/?cs=4963 |archive-date=4 October 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

==Early life==
Ryan attended high school at Marist College Eastwood and graduated with the class of 2005. He was a [[North Ryde, New South Wales#Sport and recreation|North Ryde Hawks]] junior from the age of eight.

==Rugby league career==
===2008-2010: Early playing career===
Ryan played with the [[Wests Tigers]] in 2008 season of the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]]. After 2008, Ryan played for Balmain in [[New South Wales Cup]] for 2 seasons.<ref name="smh aug 2012">{{cite news|author=Glenn Jackson|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Ryan squares off with house mate as he tries to tame Tigers|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/ryan-squares-off-with-house-mate-as-he-tries-to-tame-tigers-20120802-23iej.html|accessdate=11 August 2012|date=3 August 2012}}</ref>
[[File:Matt Ryan Balmain.jpg |thumb|right|Ryan playing for the Balmain Tigers]]

===2011: Move to Parramatta===
Ryan came to Parramatta midway through the [[2011 NRL season]], training with the first grade squad and playing in [[NSW Cup]] for feeder club [[Wentworthville Magpies|Wentworthville]]. He said of the change of clubs, "Parramatta gave me an opportunity to go across there and be coached by [[Stephen Kearney|Mooks]]. I couldn't say no. I always knew, in the back of my mind, that I was never really in the [[Wests Tigers|Tigers']] plans. They had a good quality back row there. Nothing against the club, but I just had to go somewhere for a challenge, and change my attitude a bit."<ref name="smh aug 2012" />

===2012: First grade debut===
Ryan made his [[NRL]] début in round 7 of the [[2012 NRL season]] against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]]. Ryan scored his first try in round 8 against the [[Wests Tigers]] in his second first grade appearance.  Ryan made a total of 17 appearances in his first season at Parramatta as the club finished last on the table for the first time since 1972.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2012/10/02/season-review-parramatta-eels/|title=Season review: Parramatta Eels|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2 October 2012}}</ref>

Ryan later re-signed with the [[Parramatta Eels]] for two years, until the end of the 2014 season.<ref>[http://www.parraeels.com.au/playerprofiledisplay/Eels/Matthew-Ryan/5218 Profile at Parramatta Eels]</ref>

===2013===
In June 2013, Ryan was one of 12 [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] players that were told that their futures at the club were uncertain by coach, [[Ricky Stuart]].  Ryan made a total of 7 appearances for Parramatta in the [[2013 NRL season]] as the club finished last for a second consecutive year.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-work-to-erase-bitter-memories-of-2013s-wooden-spoon-season/news-story/53c0bcf5d4870490a3ab9c448bfae244/|title=Parramatta Eels work to erase bitter memories of 2013's wooden spoon season|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/about/official-player-numbers/|title=Official Player Numbers|website=Parramatta Eels}}</ref>

===2014===
In 2014, Ryan travelled up to the Northern Hemisphere to play for [[Super League]] club, the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].

===Bradford Bulls===
Ryan joined the [[Bradford Bulls]] for the rest of the 2015 season.

'''2015''' – [[2015 Bradford Bulls season|2015]]

Ryan played in Qualifier 1 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]) to Qualifier 2 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]) then in Qualifier 5 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) to Qualifier 7 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). He scored against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (1 try).

Statistics do not include pre-season friendlies.

===Guildford Owls===
On 28 August 2017, Ryan was selected in the 2017 [[Ron Massey Cup]] team of the year.  In 2019, Ryan was selected to be the captain of Guildford for the 2019 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2017/08/27/_2017_team_of_the_ye.html|title = 2017 Team of the Year | Ron Massey Cup|date = 28 August 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailyliberal.com.au/story/6205077/owls-chasing-redemption-after-embarrassing-decider-performance/|title=Guildford Owls name star-studded side for Challenge Cup final at Dubbo|website=Daily Liberal|date=7 June 2019}}</ref>
